title: Account Vault, Explicit Activation, And Token Health
source_section: Phase 4: Account Vault And Explicit Activation
scope: Store managed Codex auth profiles and activate them by global auth symlink.
Keep import and backup flows as migration or diagnostics paths.
Make token expiration and invalid credentials visible before activation.
non_goals: Do not implement automatic account rotation yet.
Do not launch Team workers through account overlays yet.
Do not copy one profile auth over another profile auth.
Do not overwrite native auth without explicit user action.

tasks.title: Define vault layout and metadata schema
tasks.details: Define managed profile metadata, backup metadata, source path, managed path, fingerprint, and last activation fields.

tasks.title: Import current auth
tasks.details: Import the current native Codex auth.json into a named managed profile.

tasks.title: Import auth from path
tasks.details: Import another auth file after validation and source path recording.

tasks.title: Activate profile by global auth symlink
tasks.details: Link global ~/.codex/auth.json to the selected managed profile auth.json; move aside existing unmanaged native auth before linking.

tasks.title: Add token health states
tasks.details: Support ok, expiring_soon, expired, invalid, refresh_failed, rate_limited, and unknown.

tasks.title: Refresh before activation
tasks.details: Attempt refresh when refresh_token exists; keep current profile active if refresh succeeds.

tasks.title: Mark refresh failures safely
tasks.details: Mark refresh_failed without overwriting native auth, and show re-login or switch recommendation.

tasks.title: Add account UI actions
tasks.details: Added the remaining visible account UI action controls: Open Source + Validate (renamed from Test) on per-account cards.

acceptance: Activation updates the global auth symlink instead of copying profile auth.
Existing unmanaged native auth is preserved by moving it aside before linking.
UI shows exact global auth path and symlink target.
Invalid auth cannot replace the global auth link.
Expired or invalid tokens are visible as blocking account health states.
A failed token refresh never silently overwrites active auth.

implementation_notes.summary: Added vault contract constants and metadata templates in `src/host/account-manager.js`, including vault layout (`accounts`/`backups` and file names), token-health states, and profile/backup metadata schema shapes for future import/activation slices.

Implemented explicit current-auth import flow by wiring a webview account import action to pass a user-provided profile name, creating `importCurrentAuthToProfile` on the panel host, and adding `importCurrentAuthAsProfile` in account-manager to import `~/.codex/auth.json` (and sibling `config.toml` when present) into managed vault metadata with source/auth path tracking.

Added explicit auth-file import workflow from UI (`importCodexAccountFromFile` message path) through to account-manager with auth JSON validation and optional config.toml import, including source-path capture in managed profile metadata via `sourceAuthPath`.

Implemented symlink activation in `activateAccountForCodex`: global `~/.codex/auth.json` is linked to the selected managed profile auth. Existing unmanaged native `auth.json` is moved aside to `accounts/<name>/backups/` with metadata before linking.

Added bounded token health classification in `src/host/account-manager.js` by deriving `tokenHealth` from existing metadata and JWT/credential checks, and exposing it through `readAccountsForPayload` accountDetails.

Updated `activateAccountForCodex` preflight to refresh profiles that include refresh tokens before activation, block activation on non-skipped refresh failure, and persist token-health refresh results via `writeMeta` (`refresh_failed` / `refresh_succeeded`).

Handled refresh-failure blocking by persisting `refresh_failed` state and user-facing recommendation to re-login or switch profiles, returning `recommendation` on activation failure, and surfacing it in `activateCodexAccount` error UI without writing native auth.

Added an account card `Open Source` action in `src/webview-template.js` that appears for managed profiles with source auth path and dispatches a `openLocalFile` message to open the source auth json directly from the webview.

Renamed the existing account credential check button from `Test` to `Validate` while keeping the same `probeCodexAccount` message path, satisfying the Validate action requirement with no backend changes.

verification_results.command: node --check src/host/account-manager.js && node --check src/panel.js && node --check src/host/panel-view.js && node --check src/webview-template.js
